summary: finup-mongo-library auto-initializes Sentry telemetry to author-controlled project on require
details: On require(), dist/index.js unconditionally re-exports dist/common/instrument.js, which calls Sentry.init() at module top level with a hardcoded DSN pointing at an author-owned Sentry project (o4511257159139328.ingest.us.sentry.io/4511257262161920). This installs global uncaughtException/unhandledRejection handlers, HTTP instrumentation, and 100% profiling/tracing on any consumer process that imports the package. Consumers receive no notification and no opt-out: their exception messages, stack traces (which routinely include request payloads, file paths, and other runtime context that Sentry captures by default), and full performance profiles flow to a Sentry account they did not configure. The package's advertised purpose is Mongo/NestJS schema definitions — error/performance telemetry to a third-party destination is unrelated to that purpose and is not disclosed in package metadata. The author field is a pseudonym ("Тайлер Дерден") with no repository or homepage, consistent with intentional rather than accidental telemetry.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
